topic.title: Diagnostics: GuestMemory Store-Watch and Retire-Trace Arm

header.para: Diagnostics: GuestMemory Store-Watch and Retire-Trace Arm

body.para: Two observe-only instruments placed at the GuestMemory commit sink -- the universal point through which every committed DRAM byte passes. They capture stores (and arm a full executed-instruction trace) that the pipeline-level EMULATR_PA_WATCH cannot see, because some firmware stores (notably the HWRPB construction at PA 0x2000) reach DRAM by a path that bypasses the MemDrainer drain-watch. EmulatR is the tool of record for firmware execution analysis; these instruments produce a faithful, deterministic capture without Ghidra.

These instruments are compiled in only when EMULATR_DIAGNOSTIC_LOGGING is defined -- that is, in a Debug or RelWithDebInfo build. In a Release build they compile to nothing (zero bytes, zero cycles), so the faithful production path is untouched. Build RelWithDebInfo for diagnostics. To confirm the code is present, set EMULATR_GMEM_WATCH=0x2058 on a cold boot; a GMEM-WATCH line on stderr proves it is compiled in.
Sink store-watch -- set EMULATR_GMEM_WATCH=<pa>. Logs every store whose byte range overlaps the watched quadword, at the GuestMemory sink. Output: GMEM-WATCH(0x<pa>) STORE pa=0x.. sz=N v=0x...

Store-triggered retire-trace arm -- set EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_PA=<pa> (optionally EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_VAL=<v> and EMULATR_TRACE_ARM_INSTRS=<n>). When a store to that address (and matching value, if given) commits, the windowed retire trace is armed for N retired instructions (default 8,000,000), reusing the existing .trc machinery. Output marker: GMEM-TRACE-ARM pa=0x.. v=0x.. -> retire window N instrs.

table.style: border-width:0px; border-spacing:0px; border-collapse:collapse; cell-border-width:1px; border-color:#000000; border-style:solid; head-row-background-color:#00ffff; alt-row-background-color:none;

td.para: CONFIGURE-TIME (CMake), not env. PipelineDriver's per-commit callback (sink->onCommit) is compiled OUT by default; without this flag the .trc opens but never fills. REQUIRED for any retire trace.
Construct the windowed DecListingSink (.trc open, emission window-gated). REQUIRED for the trace-arm instrument.
Output directory for the .trc / breakpoint / profiler trace files.
Arm trigger = the HWRPB self-pointer store (build_hwrpb entry).
Arm only when the stored value equals this (skips the memset zero-fill of the same address).
Retire-window length in instructions; tune to cover the region of interest.

Alternate arm at the IIC bus (the MMIO path the DRAM sink cannot see). Fires once on the first START transaction; 0/1 = any node, else only when (addr & 0xFE) matches (e.g. 0x40 = first status-controller probe = iic_init at ~cyc 185M). Use to capture iic_init registration.
Close the window on a store to this PA (+ optional EMULATR_TRACE_DISARM_VAL). Backstop bound for an IIC-armed window: 0x2000/VAL=0x2000 stops at the HWRPB base store (build_hwrpb entry).
Log the SYSVAR store (HWRPB+0x58) at the sink.
Correlate the fopen("iic_ocp0") path with IIC bus activity.
With rm -f ds20_flash.rom: factory-fresh cold NVRAM.
Optional: silicon (REAL_HW) path; unset = ISP (virtual) path. See Virtualization Methods.

para.text: cmake --build out/build/<relwithdebinfo-dir> # NOT a Release config

body.para: The .trc in EMULATR_RETIRE_TRACE_DIR holds the executed instruction stream from build_hwrpb entry onward -- including get_sysvar() -> fopen("iic_ocp0") -> the member decision -> the SYSVAR store. The GMEM-WATCH(0x2058) line gives the SYSVAR store; correlate it against addr=0x40 in the IIC trace to determine whether the open reached the bus. Faithfulness note: these instruments are observe-only and the guest is cycle-deterministic across builds, so the RelWithDebInfo capture reflects the same guest behavior Release would produce.
See Also. Virtualization Methods; CMake Macro Guards; HWRPB Key/Value Offset Map. What is EmulatR
